title: Fluentd Memory Buffer Utilization Approaching Limit
app: Fluentd monitor agent, Prometheus exporter optional
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| eval used=coalesce(buffer_queue_length, buffer_stage_length, queue_length)
| eval lim=coalesce(buffer_total_limit, buffer_chunk_limit, total_limit)
| eval pct=round(100*used/nullif(lim,0),1)
| where pct > 75 OR used > 0 AND lim-used < 500
| stats latest(pct) as buf_pct, latest(used) as q, latest(lim) as cap by host, plugin_id
| sort -buf_pct
description: Memory buffers protect against short downstream outages; running near the ceiling leaves no headroom for bursts and precedes blocking `emit` calls. Percent-of-limit tracking triggers proactive scaling.
value: Avoids tail drops on bursty services like ingress controllers during attacks or viral traffic.
implementation: Some plugins expose only chunk counts—map to bytes via avg chunk size calibration. Combine with `sourcetype=fluentd:log` overflow messages for corroboration.
visualization: Gauge charts per plugin, heatmap (host × buffer pct), alert table.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full the in-memory holding area is before logs are written to disk or sent onward. If it is almost full, you are one spike away from trouble.
